title: Data Display
intro: The read-only primitives: DataItem and DataItemRow render labeled facts in drawers, headers, and detail panels; NothingMessage owns the empty state and LoadingAnimation owns the fetch state. None of these take a `value` prop — the value is always children.

blocks.body:
Reach for this family whenever a surface shows facts instead of collecting them. DataItem stacks a hint-gray label over a value for summary strips and drawer headers; DataItemRow puts label and value on one line for vertical field lists. NothingMessage is the one sanctioned empty state — every empty grid, list, and no-results surface uses it, never a hand-rolled centered div. LoadingAnimation covers full-area fetches with a Lottie animation.

The single most important fact about this family: the value slot is `children`. There is no `value` prop on DataItem or DataItemRow — passing one renders an empty value cell with no warning.

blocks.title: DataItem — stacked label over value
blocks.caption: Text and numeric values: a 0.9em hint-gray label (Texto `label`/`hint`) over a 1.1em value (Texto `p2`). The value is passed as children — the `value=` prop strings printed on the demo cards are wrong; that prop does not exist.

blocks.title: DataItem props
blocks.intro: The published component wraps both lines in Texto: label as `category='label' appearance='hint'` (class `detail-data-label pb-1`), value as `category='p2'` (class `detail-data-value`).

rows.notes: The hint-gray caption line above the value.
The value. This is the value slot — there is no `value` prop.
Inline style on the value line — weight or color emphasis without replacing the Texto wrapper.
Spread onto the label element — tooltips, data attributes, click handlers.
Class on the wrapper div. Use for spacing within a strip; prefer the parent's `gap`.

blocks.title: DataItemRow — single-line field rows
blocks.caption: Label and value share one line on a 12/12 antd column split. In narrow containers long labels wrap ("Effective Date", right) — tune labelSpan/valueSpan instead of letting labels break.

blocks.title: DataItemRow props
blocks.intro: An antd Row with two Cols on the 24-column grid. Same Texto treatment as DataItem: hint label, p2 value.

rows.notes: Required. Left column content — accepts nodes, so labels can carry an info icon.
Required. The value, in the right column. No `value` prop exists.
Label column width out of 24. Drop to 8-10 in wide panels so values get room.
Value column width out of 24. labelSpan + valueSpan should total 24.
Spread onto the label Col — alignment, offset, responsive spans.
Class on the outer Row.

rows.name: DataItem
rows.when: Facts read left-to-right: summary strips, drawer headers, KPI clusters. Label stacks over value.

rows.name: DataItemRow
rows.when: Facts read top-to-bottom: vertical field lists in detail panels and drawers. Label and value share a line.

rows.name: NothingMessage
rows.when: Every empty grid, list, or no-results state. The only sanctioned empty state — both title and message are required.
rows.code: <NothingMessage title='No Results' message='No contracts match your filters' />

rows.name: LoadingAnimation
rows.when: Full-area data fetches that deserve a Lottie animation. For lightweight inline waits, wrap content in Overlay instead.

rows.name: NotificationMessage
rows.when: Never. Deprecated and flagged for removal — call the antd `notification` API directly.

blocks.title: Canonical usage
blocks.code:
import { DataItem, DataItemRow, NothingMessage, Horizontal, Vertical } from '@gravitate-js/excalibrr'

// Summary strip — the value is ALWAYS children, never a `value` prop
<Horizontal gap={24}>
  <DataItem label='Contract ID'>CTR-2024-001</DataItem>
  <DataItem label='Total Volume'>12,500 gal</DataItem>
  <DataItem label='Rack Avg'>$2.4500/gal</DataItem>
</Horizontal>

// Detail panel field list — widen the value column when labels are short
<Vertical gap={4}>
  <DataItemRow label='Status'>Active</DataItemRow>
  <DataItemRow label='Effective Date' labelSpan={8} valueSpan={16}>
    01/15/2024
  </DataItemRow>
</Vertical>

// Empty state — title AND message are required
{rows.length === 0 && (
  <NothingMessage title='No Results' message='No contracts match your filters' />
)}
blocks.note: Spacing comes from Horizontal/Vertical gap props, never style objects. Money copy is decimal dollars ($2.4500/gal), never cents symbols.

blocks.title: NothingMessage — empty states
blocks.caption: Three correct uses — no data, no search results, and first-run — each with the fixed NoResultsIcon, a bold 1.4em title, and a supporting message. The icon is built in and cannot be swapped.

blocks.title: NothingMessage props
blocks.intro: Exactly three props. The icon is the library's NoResultsIcon — there is no icon, action, or size prop.

rows.notes: Required. Bold 1.4em headline in var(--gray-600). CSS capitalizes every word — write it short: "No Results", "No Items".
Required. Centered supporting line — say what would fill the space or how to fix the filter.
Appended to `nothing-container` for positioning overrides.

blocks.title: LoadingAnimation props
blocks.intro: Lottie-driven full-area loading state, used for page and panel fetches. It has no built-in animation — you must supply the Lottie JSON, or it renders a blank 355x245 box. (The live showcase makes exactly that mistake, so there is no specimen here.)

rows.notes: Required. The animation source — import a .json Lottie file. Without it the component renders empty.
Required. Bold headline under the animation — h5, or h1 when `large`.
Required. Supporting line — p1, or h5 when `large`.
Scales the text tier up (title h1, message h5) for full-page loads.
Set false for one-shot animations like a success check.
Animation width in px.
Animation height in px.

blocks.title: NotificationMessage — deprecated toasts
blocks.caption: Error (WarningFilled in var(--theme-error)) and success (CheckCircleFilled in var(--theme-success)) toasts, top-right. Shown for recognition only — the source flags this function as deprecated; new code calls antd `notification` directly.

blocks.title: Type and color hooks
blocks.intro: Values from library source. The class names are stable hooks for the rare targeted override.

rows.use: Label line of DataItem and DataItemRow.
Value line of DataItem and DataItemRow.
Centers the empty state inside a height-constrained flex parent.

rows.value: padded column (p-5) wrapping Lottie + title + message

items.do: Pass the value as children: <DataItem label='Status'>Active</DataItem>
items.dont: Pass a value prop: <DataItem label='Status' value='Active' />
items.why: No `value` prop exists on DataItem or DataItemRow — it is silently dropped and the value cell renders empty.

items.do: Use NothingMessage for every empty grid, list, and no-results state, with both title and message.
items.dont: Hand-roll centered divs with inline styles, or pass only a message.
items.why: One component keeps every empty state identical; a missing title renders a blank bold line above the message.

items.do: Put NothingMessage inside a height-constrained flex parent (Vertical flex='1' or height='100%') when it should center vertically.
items.dont: Drop it into an auto-height wrapper and expect centering.
items.why: The container relies on flex: 1 + height: 100% — in an auto-height parent it hugs its content at the top.

items.do: Gray emphasis on values via valueStyle or Texto appearance='medium'.
items.dont: Wrap values in Texto appearance='secondary' for gray.
items.why: secondary is BLUE (var(--theme-color-2)); medium is the gray (var(--gray-500)).

items.title: The value slot is children — `value` does not exist
items.detail: Both DataItem and DataItemRow type the value as children. A `value` prop is silently ignored and the cell renders empty. The Data Display showcase itself makes this mistake — its printed prop strings (`value="CTR-2024-001"`) are wrong; trust this reference, not the demo card captions.

items.title: NothingMessage takes exactly title, message, className
items.detail: There is no `icon` or `actions` prop — the NoResultsIcon is hard-coded, and extra props are silently ignored (the welcome-page example passes both and gets neither). Need a call-to-action under an empty state? Render a GraviButton as a sibling below it.

items.title: nothing-title capitalizes every word
items.detail: `.nothing-title` carries `text-transform: capitalize`, so "no results found" renders as "No Results Found". Write short Title-Case titles and keep sentence-case prose in the message line.

items.title: LoadingAnimation renders blank without animationData
items.detail: There is no default animation. Omit `animationData` and you get an empty 355x245 box with no error — the live Feedback showcase demonstrates the failure. Import a Lottie .json and pass it explicitly, along with the required title and message.

items.title: NotificationMessage is deprecated
items.detail: Library source flags it 'unused, will be removed'. It is a plain function (message, description, showErrorMessage) wrapping antd's static notification.open — not a component. New code calls the antd `notification` API directly.

items.title: DataItemRow's 12/12 split wastes space in wide panels
items.detail: The default gives the label half the row. In drawers and panels wider than ~400px, set labelSpan={8} valueSpan={16} so values do not start at the midline and long labels do not wrap.
